description: Execute a plan until it is complete

steps.label: Planner Select And Store Next Plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Re-read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first and determine the active `plan_path`, then re-open that exact relative `plan_path` from disk even if you have read it before. Re-check current repository branch state directly from git, for example with `git branch --show-current`, and check the most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git log --oneline -1`. You MUST double-check to see if another agent has edited the plan or changed its scope since you last looked at it. You MUST read the whole plan again and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of what the plan entails.

Do you have any new requirement/scope based questions about the plan that need clarification before the plan is tasked up or worked on? Even if you have previously provided questions, maybe you have thought of some more after re-reading the plan and asking yourself `Am I sure about the full plan scope and requirements, and is it all documented within the plan`? If a '## Questions' section doesn't already exist within the plan, then create one at the end of the file, even if you do not have any questions to add. If you have questions not already in the section, then add them to the '## Questions' section as a numbered list. If the section already contains questions, then please renumber them so that the full list is ordered numerically and starts from 1. If you do not have any new questions you MUST write the exact line '- No Further Questions' immediately below the '## Questions' title, and above any existing questions within the section.

steps.label: Check for No Further Questions
steps.question: Did you add additional/new questions to the plan file (adding 'No Further Questions' does NOT count as adding questions)? You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for No Further Questions
steps.question: Re-open the plan that you are working on, ensuring that you re-read it from disk since another agent will have edited it, so what you think the plan contains is definitely out of date. Does the plan contain the line 'No Further Questions' within the '## Questions' section of the plan file? You MUST re-read the plan before giving your answer as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

content: Please read through each question listed within the '## Questions' section of the plan as it should have been edited by another agent. For each question, I want you to perform thorough research for each question separately in order. While researching an answer, I want you to follow these golden rules: 1. Use the KISS principle, only planning what is needed for THIS story and avoiding extra work and over engineering. Follow established patterns of what has been done before. 3. Ensure we are not going outside the scope of the original plan. 4. Ensure everything we are planning to do has a purpose - To answer each question you MUST follow these steps: 1. Use the code_info tool to find out how we have approached similar issues within this repository before.  2. Use the code_info tool to find out how these kinds of issues have been approached within ALL ingested repositories. 3. Use the deepwiki and context7 tools along with web searches to confirm any questions related to external libraries that are not accessible from the code_info tool.  4. Search the web to see if other people have asked the same question, and use the best and proven solutions that match with our KISS principles.  - Once you have an answer to a question, add it to a '## Decisions' section immediately after the '## Questions' section (you will need to create it if it doesn't already exist). Each decision should be added as an item within a numbered list under the '## Decisions' section. The decision should indicate: - the question being addressed, WHY the question matters, WHAT the answer is, WHERE the answer came from and WHY it is the best answer to the question. In addition to adding the answer to the '## Decisions' section, you MUST update all other sections of the plan where appropriate based on the information from the answer, including description, acceptance, out of scope, etc... You MUST ALSO remove answered questions from the '## Questions' section, but never actually remove the '## Questions' section heading (in case more questions are added later). If it is impossible for you to answer a question after following the above steps, just leave it within the '## Questions' section. Never task up and commit after making any changes.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after planning

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Then commit all local changes, and if possible, git push the story branch in the current repository and in every repository listed in `additional_repositories`.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after improve plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk now and read the whole selected plan again before judging the tasking output, because another agent has just edited it. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. The plan has now been tasked up. Confirm the tasking output against the freshly re-read plan on disk, including any new tasks, subtasks, testing sections, and documentation locations that were just added. Please check that the tasks created fully cover that selected plan and that nothing has been missed. If you feel something is missing, please update the plan and tasks accordingly. Remember to ensure that each task has clear implementation instructions and testing steps so a developer can fully understand what to do. Please confirm once you are happy that the plan has been fully tasked up and nothing is missing. Do not ask me any questions, instead just update the plan and tasks following the KISS principle until you are happy everything is fully covered.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after tasking

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then check the last commit in the current repository and in every additional repository AND re-read that same plan, even if you have read it before. You MUST double-check to see if tasks have been added to it by another agent or if anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the plan and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. You MUST read the whole plan again even if you have read it before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of the remaining work.

steps.label: Task Execution And Blocker Repair Loop
Check for blocker before automated proof
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Scan the plan from bottom to top and identify the highest-numbered task whose `Task Status` is either `__in_progress__` or `__done__`. Does that task currently contain a standalone Implementation Notes entry whose first token is exactly `**BLOCKER**`? Ignore inline references to `**BLOCKER**`, ignore `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and ignore historical notes titled `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Run automated proof and fix issues
Check for blocker before manual testing
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Scan the plan from bottom to top and identify the highest-numbered task whose `Task Status` is either `__in_progress__` or `__done__`. Does that task currently contain a standalone Implementation Notes entry whose first token is exactly `**BLOCKER**`? Ignore inline references to `**BLOCKER**`, ignore `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and ignore historical notes titled `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for blocker after manual testing
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Scan the plan from bottom to top and identify the highest-numbered task whose `Task Status` is either `__in_progress__` or `__done__`. Does that task currently contain a standalone Implementation Notes entry whose first token is exactly `**BLOCKER**`? Ignore inline references to `**BLOCKER**`, ignore `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and ignore historical notes titled `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check if manual testing added follow-up work
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Scan the plan from bottom to top and identify the highest-numbered task whose `Task Status` is either `__done__` or `__in_progress__`. Did your latest manual testing run add any new unchecked subtasks or new unchecked testing steps to that task? Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Research blocker solution and prove it
Refresh current plan handoff after planner edits

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and open only the relative `plan_path` stored there in the current repository. Do not rediscover the story independently. Before answering completion, list each task's Task Status (including the final task). Explicitly state whether the final task is __done__ or not, cite the relative plan file path you read, and state whether every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename or whether repository branch scope has drifted.

steps.label: Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then re-read that plan and check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository. You MUST confirm the final task's Task Status from the plan file and only answer yes if every task is marked __done__ and every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. Is the plan that was being worked on now complete? You MUST re-read the plan and check the last 3 git commits before giving your answer as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after review
Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then re-read that plan and check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository. Is the plan that was being worked on now complete and do all repositories in scope remain on branches whose story numbers match the selected plan filename? You MUST re-read the plan and check the last 3 git commits before giving your answer, as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.
